message:
fix(fp-types): semantic_eq bridges all Null kinds (br-frankenpandas-pxv5)

73 of 108 fp-conformance bootstrap-suite fixture failures share the
pattern 'actual=Null(NaN), expected=Null(Null)': fp-frame normalizes
Float64 column missing cells to Null(NaN) at Column::new time (the
dtype-specific missing marker), while fixture oracles encode the
canonical missing marker as Null(Null). semantic_eq previously
bridged Null(NaN) <-> Float64::NAN but fell through to derived
PartialEq for cross-kind Null <-> Null pairs, rejecting them despite
both representing missingness.

Adds (Null(_), Null(_)) => true arm before the PartialEq fallback.
All Null variants (Null / NaN / NaT) are semantically missing and
should compare equal for oracle-parity checks; keeps PartialEq
available for non-Null cases.

Impact: packet_suite_is_green_for_bootstrap_cases case-level results
go from 1141/108 passed/failed to 1212/37 — 71 fixtures recovered in
one change, across dataframe_abs/astype/diff/rank/round/skew/value_counts,
series_dt_* operations, dataframe_resample/rolling mean/sum with
nulls, and dataframe_pct_change/memory_usage with_nulls variants.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -111,6 +111,13 @@ impl Scalar {
             }
             (Self::Null(NullKind::NaN), Self::Float64(v))
             | (Self::Float64(v), Self::Null(NullKind::NaN)) => v.is_nan(),
+            // All Null kinds (Null / NaN / NaT) mark missingness; they are
+            // semantically indistinguishable for oracle-parity checks even
+            // though derived PartialEq would reject a cross-kind pair.
+            // fp-frame normalizes Float64 column missing cells to
+            // Null(NaN) at Column::new time, while fixture oracles encode
+            // the canonical missing marker as Null(Null).
+            (Self::Null(_), Self::Null(_)) => true,
             _ => self == other,
         }
     }
